title: 33GOD CloudEvents Base Envelope (v1)
description: CloudEvents 1.0 envelope with 33GOD extension fields. The 'type' regex and extension fields (kind, actor, ordering_key, command_id, idempotency_key, delivery) enforce the Bloodbank Event Naming Contract v1 — see bloodbank/docs/event-naming.md. Per ADR-0001, this is the wire-level shape for all v3 events published through Dapr + NATS JetStream. Legacy v2 events (RabbitMQ path) continue to use base_event.v1.json.

specversion.description: CloudEvents specification version. Always '1.0' for v3.

id.description: Unique identifier for this event. Consumers dedup on this. Set by producer; Dapr preserves.

source.description: Event source as URI-reference. Convention: 'urn:33god:service:<service-name>' or 'urn:33god:cli:<tool-name>' for operator-issued events. Provider names ARE allowed here; the banned-token rule applies only to 'type'.

type.description: Bloodbank Event Naming Contract v1 type. Shape: bloodbank.v<N>.<domain>.<entity>.<action>. Exactly 5 dotted tokens; domain/entity/action come from the allowlists in bloodbank/docs/event-naming.md §6-§8. Provider/CLI/model names are BANNED here and live in 'actor' instead. The NATS subject is bloodbank.<kind>.v<N>.<domain>.<entity>.<action> where <kind> in {evt, cmd, rpy}.

subject.description: Canonical NATS subject bound to type and kind: bloodbank.<evt|cmd|rpy>.v<N>.<domain>.<entity>.<action>. Required by the Bloodbank contract.

time.description: RFC3339 UTC timestamp when the event was produced. Preserved verbatim on replay.

datacontenttype.description: MIME type of the data field. 33GOD default is 'application/json'.

dataschema.description: Apicurio registry URI identifying the canonical envelope schema. Convention: 'apicurio://holyfields/<type>/versions/<n>'; registered same-type command/reply variants append '.command' or '.reply' to the artifact identity only.

correlationid.description: Correlation ID for causal chains. All events in one logical workflow share the same correlationid.

causationid.description: ID of the CloudEvent that directly caused this one. The field is mandatory; a root-event schema may allow null or a producer may self-reference its stable event ID. Together with correlationid forms a DAG of causation.

producer.description: Canonical producer identity. Agent name, service ID, or 'operator:<name>' for human-issued events. Provider names allowed here; only 'type' is restricted.

service.description: The service that emitted this event. Matches the service registry entry name.

domain.description: The domain this event belongs to. MUST match segment 3 of 'type' for Bloodbank v1 events.

schemaref.description: Short schema reference, distinct from dataschema (which is the full URI). Normally '<type>.v<N>'; registered same-type command/reply variants append '.command' or '.reply' before the schema version.

traceparent.description: W3C Trace Context traceparent header. Zero-trace placeholder is '00-00000000000000000000000000000000-0000000000000000-00'.

kind.description: Bloodbank Event Naming Contract v1 §4 discriminator. MUST match the kind marker in the NATS subject (evt|cmd|rpy).

actor.description: Who/what generated this fact, normalized for provider-agnostic consumption. See bloodbank/docs/event-naming.md §10. Provider/CLI/model identity lives here, NEVER in 'type'.

agent_id.description: Stable canonical identifier of the actor (e.g. 'bloodbank.agent.claude').

cli.description: CLI binary name when actor.type == 'agent_cli'. Null otherwise.

ordering_key.description: Stable ordering bucket for events on the same logical entity. Convention: 'thread:<id>' | 'turn:<id>' | 'invocation:<id>' | 'cli_session:<id>' | 'process:<id>'. Required for kind=event; not used for commands/replies.

command_id.description: Unique command identifier. Stable across retries — the SAME command_id appears on every retry. Required for kind=command.

idempotency_key.description: Stable key for the command's effect. Convention: '<action>:<entity-scope>'. Bloodbank dedupes on (idempotency_key, command_id). Required for kind=command.

delivery.description: Command delivery semantics. v1 supports single_consumer only. Required for kind=command.

data.description: Domain-specific event payload. Validated separately by the domain schema identified by dataschema / schemaref.
